title: Golden Globe Best Motion Picture – Drama Predictions
text: The article covers predictions for the 83rd Annual Golden Globes ceremony focusing on the Best Motion Picture – Drama category. Sinners leads with a 53% probability of winning, according to Kalshi prediction markets. The horror drama directed by Ryan Coogler explores themes of the Great Migration, racism, and the Black community's struggle for freedom and has secured seven Golden Globe nominations. Hamnet, directed by Chloé Zhao, follows with 39% odds. The film features Jessie Buckley and Paul Mescal in a dramatization of the Shakespeares grieving their son's death. Other nominees include Sentimental Value, a Norwegian film with 8% odds about two sisters who reconnect with their estranged father, a filmmaker; It Was Just an Accident, an Iran-France-Luxembourg co-production with 5% odds and winner of the 2025 Palme d'Or; Frankenstein, a Guillermo del Toro adaptation with five nominations; and The Secret Agent, a Brazilian drama. The article notes this category reflects an increasingly global and diverse awards season.
